---
title: Munich Traffic Accidents
---

Munich's [Monatszahlen Verkehrsunfälle](https://opendata.muenchen.de/dataset/monatszahlen-verkehrsunfaelle)
records monthly road-accident counts published by the Statistisches Amt München,
going back to the 1990s. This demo downloads the full-history CSV from the open
data portal and upserts it into a Delta Lake table using the
[`@incremental`](../reference/incremental.incremental.qmd) and
[`@schema`](../reference/schema.schema.qmd) decorators — a pattern suited to
"snapshot-replacement" sources where the supplier rewrites the whole file each month.

Three categories are tracked: total accidents, alcohol-related accidents, and
hit-and-run (*Fluchtunfälle*).

**Data source:** Statistisches Amt München ·
License: [Datenlizenz Deutschland – Namensnennung 2.0](https://www.govdata.de/dl-de/by-2-0)

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
from pathlib import Path

import altair as alt
import polars as pl

_env = Path("..") / ".env"
if _env.exists():
    for _line in _env.read_text().splitlines():
        if _line and not _line.startswith("#") and "=" in _line:
            _k, _, _v = _line.partition("=")
            os.environ.setdefault(_k.strip(), _v.strip())

BUCKET = os.environ.get("MUNICH_CYCLING_BUCKET", "data-warehousing-with-polars")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
df = (
    pl.scan_delta(f"s3://{BUCKET}/delta/munich_accidents")
    .select("MONATSZAHL", "AUSPRAEGUNG", "JAHR", "MONAT", "WERT", "date")
    .filter(pl.col("AUSPRAEGUNG") == "insgesamt")
    .collect()
    .sort("date")
)
df.head(5)
```

## Monthly trend by accident type

Total accidents per month since records began. The 28-day rolling mean
highlights the long-term trend and smooths over seasonal noise.

```{python}
#| echo: false
totals = (
    df.filter(pl.col("MONATSZAHL") == "Verkehrsunfälle")
    .drop_nulls("WERT")
    .sort("date")
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("WERT").rolling_mean(window_size=12).alias("rolling_12m")
    )
)

base = alt.Chart(totals).encode(x=alt.X("date:T", title="Month"))

raw = base.mark_line(opacity=0.3, color="#e45756").encode(
    y=alt.Y("WERT:Q", title="Accidents per month"),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("date:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("WERT:Q", title="Accidents", format=",.0f"),
    ],
)
smooth = base.mark_line(color="#e45756", strokeWidth=2).encode(
    y="rolling_12m:Q",
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("date:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("rolling_12m:Q", title="12-month avg", format=",.0f"),
    ],
)

(raw + smooth).properties(
    width="container", height=260, title="Total accidents (Verkehrsunfälle insgesamt)"
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```

## All three categories

Comparing total accidents, alcohol-related incidents, and hit-and-run cases on the
same timeline (12-month rolling average, so the values are on different scales —
use the category to select/deselect).

```{python}
#| echo: false
multi = (
    df.filter(
        pl.col("MONATSZAHL").is_in(
            ["Verkehrsunfälle", "Alkoholunfälle", "Fluchtunfälle"]
        )
    )
    .drop_nulls("WERT")
    .sort("MONATSZAHL", "date")
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("WERT")
        .rolling_mean(window_size=12)
        .over("MONATSZAHL")
        .alias("rolling_12m")
    )
)

color_scale = alt.Scale(
    domain=["Verkehrsunfälle", "Alkoholunfälle", "Fluchtunfälle"],
    range=["#e45756", "#f58518", "#4c78a8"],
)

selection = alt.selection_point(fields=["MONATSZAHL"], bind="legend")

alt.Chart(multi).mark_line(strokeWidth=2).encode(
    x=alt.X("date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("rolling_12m:Q", title="12-month rolling average"),
    color=alt.Color(
        "MONATSZAHL:N",
        scale=color_scale,
        legend=alt.Legend(title="Category"),
    ),
    opacity=alt.condition(selection, alt.value(1), alt.value(0.1)),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("date:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("MONATSZAHL:N", title="Category"),
        alt.Tooltip("rolling_12m:Q", title="12-month avg", format=",.1f"),
    ],
).add_params(selection).properties(
    width="container",
    height=280,
    title="Accident categories — 12-month rolling average",
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```

## Seasonality

Average accidents by calendar month (across all available years), showing that
winter and early-autumn months tend to see more incidents.

```{python}
#| echo: false
seasons = (
    df.filter(pl.col("MONATSZAHL") == "Verkehrsunfälle")
    .drop_nulls("WERT")
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("date").dt.month().alias("month_num"),
        pl.col("date").dt.strftime("%b").alias("month_name"),
    )
    .group_by("month_num", "month_name")
    .agg(pl.col("WERT").mean().alias("avg_accidents"))
    .sort("month_num")
)

alt.Chart(seasons).mark_bar(color="#e45756").encode(
    x=alt.X("month_name:N", sort=None, title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("avg_accidents:Q", title="Average accidents"),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("month_name:N", title="Month"),
        alt.Tooltip("avg_accidents:Q", title="Avg accidents", format=",.0f"),
    ],
).properties(
    width="container", height=220, title="Average accidents by month of year"
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```
